name: recall_pam_faillock_maps_ac7
description: RECALL case — expected to FAIL pre-RAG-rewrite. Real eMASS BoE file under 01.AC/ (CTP-017_account_lockout): a script(1) capture of /etc/security/faillock.conf and the pam.d stack. Account lockout after N failed attempts is AC-7 (Unsuccessful Logon Attempts). Body has NO control ID / CCI / doc number; pam_faillock parameter names ('deny', 'unlock_time') do not lexically overlap 'unsuccessful logon attempts'. Current tagger → zero tags. Distractor IA-5 (password policy) present as precision guard — faillock is lockout, not password complexity.

objectives.text: The information system enforces a limit of consecutive invalid logon attempts by a user during a defined time period.
objectives.implementation_guidance: Configure the system to lock an account after a threshold of failed logons.

objectives.text: The information system enforces password complexity by the minimum number of upper case characters used.

evidence.title: CTP-017 account lockout faillock capture
evidence.text:
Script started on 2026-03-05 14:02:10-0500
[root@app01 ~]# cat /etc/security/faillock.conf
# deny: lock account after this many failures
deny = 3
# fail_interval window in seconds
fail_interval = 900
# unlock_time: keep locked for this many seconds
unlock_time = 0
silent
audit
even_deny_root
[root@app01 ~]# grep faillock /etc/pam.d/system-auth
auth        required      pam_faillock.so preauth silent deny=3 unlock_time=0
auth        [default=die]  pam_faillock.so authfail deny=3 unlock_time=0
account     required      pam_faillock.so
[root@app01 ~]# faillock --user testacct
testacct:
When                Type  Source           Valid
2026-03-05 13:58:01 RHOST 10.0.0.5         V
[root@app01 ~]# exit
Script done on 2026-03-05 14:02:55-0500
